# Transcrição: 2. Attribution Trinity Example

- **Fonte:** 2. Attribution Trinity Example
- **Duração:** 06:44
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

## [00:00 - 03:03]

So now that you've watched the video on the attribution trinity, I'm going to show you a really small case study that I put together from my elite group just to show what was actually happening with the data and how we were using that for decision making. And this was a kind of set it and forget it test. We just picked out some, picked out an ad set. We set this up for iOS only and Android only campaigns. So there's absolutely no crossover because most people don't have both devices. And then just one ad running in each ad set just to see over a period of time, we don't touch it, don't do anything. What actually happens with the data that's coming through? Now over this test, we spent $1500 generated for purchases and you know ignore these stats, they're terrible. But we wanted to look at the data coming through and this is an e-commerce store for ad to car, new content, in the shape, checkout, etc, etc. At the end of this test, and this was run on an e-commerce store that was not, had no other traffic, no nothing else going on. So at the end of it, we generated four sales. And that's what Facebook told us. We then looked into the data in Google Analytics and also to Shopify and they actually told us the different story. Now the challenge right now is following the changes from Apple, the way Facebook are collecting that data has changed. One of the biggest changes, and I'll have the link below so you can read more about it, is that Facebook no longer in its algorithm and also its reporting shows real time feedback and performance. So for example, prior to the Apple changes, Facebook would look at the data and make very quick decisions on the data and you could also do the same because the data was fairly accurate, maybe not 100%, but it was fairly accurate. So I've had situations where I'm scaling up in the day, I can rely on the data, I'm looking at the data and everything's performing okay, I'm spending more, etc, etc. That's changed now. Facebook say up to 72 hours, your data could be delayed. And also the algorithm has been changed, so it's not reacting so fast. And that's sometimes where you see opportunities and things are working well and you're like, why can't Facebook just spend more money? It's because the algorithms changed. And it's limited to a big part of the learning. Data's missing, data points are missing for us to observe on what's happening. Knowledge is becoming out of date in terms of how we are. I think the biggest shift here is when Apple made this change as advertisers, it took us a bit of time to actually catch up with what was happening. And I think that's probably what's led to people making bad decisions in ads manager and with it with their spend, because the data's not there. So how do you react? Doesn't make you a bad media buyer, a bad marketer or a bad person. It just means that the parameters, you know, the goalposts have changed and we need to adapt with it. So going back to our example, and you know,

## [03:04 - 06:08]

you'll go through the training on how to set this up in Google Analytics. For this particular test, Google Analytics showed us exactly the same data that Facebook did. This is great. So, you know, we've generated four cells, two in each of the campaigns, so two from iOS and two from Android. That's great. That just means that our tracking for this test was accurate between Facebook and Google. However, what did Shopify tell us? And in this case, Shopify actually recorded three, so actually over this period, there were five cells. Shopify recorded three against the iOS campaign and two against the Android campaign. So hopefully you can see the problem with this. If Google, our analytics engine has missed a transaction and Facebook has also missed a transaction, that's a problem. So, you know, here $109 generated from that Android campaign through Google, it's matched here, but the iOS campaigns $140, $190 overall. The problem occurs when if you're looking at your optimization and you say, regardless of these numbers, let's just say, you know, the, the worth three and two purchases and that third purchase could turn that campaign from negative to positive, that decision making is super critical. Because otherwise, you might look at these and say, well, they're both performing bad, I'm going to do something, I'm going to try different audience, try different ad, etc. But what if that one extra sale or the extra 10 sales or whatever it is that have been missed by Facebook and potentially by Google Analytics, have not been captured in your decision making. And that's why where possible, I want as many data sources as possible, so that can make decisions on what to do next. And by the way, you'll go through the training and I'll show you how to look at this in Shopify. And this is part of the problem and also part of the solution. Shopify is not an analytics tool, it's not designed for you to be able to look at your web traffic and make key decisions. But they're trying. And this is actually a really good step forward. And as you'll see in the training for this section, it is a little bit clunky right now. And hopefully, at some point in the future, it'll be updated and you'll look at this video and say, Hey, deep-ish, this video is so out of date, because look, the interface is so much better on Shopify. I look forward to that day. But right now, it is not great. So some of the data and decision making is not as easy as it could be. So for example, in Shopify, you can also do breakdowns by different UTM parameters. So here, I've done a breakdown by UTM campaign. Here, I've added UTM content, which pulls in my ad set name. And for this, you can also, you'll go through the training on how to make sure this happens. But here, I can see a breakdown of exactly which ad sets and I can go down to the ad level to know

## [06:08 - 06:44]

exactly which ad generated the sale. So for iOS, for example, I can see where that sale was missed out. And that gives me an idea of where to optimize. And this is the power of making sure you've got the attribution trinity set up. The more data and decision decision making points you can pull from, the better the decisions you can make. So that's the real key takeaway from this is the attribution trinity is all about decision making and being able to put the right dollar in the right place to maximize performance.
